generated_comment: I have no issue with women doing well, but these days every company wants to showcase one female face at the top for optics. Many genuinely capable men are overlooked, and then if the woman succeeds everyone says empowerment, if she fails nobody questions how she was selected in the first place. Real merit should matter, not quota and PR.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines women leaders' merit by implying they are promoted for diversity optics rather than competence. It encodes the 'diversity hire/quota' surface manifestation in a polished meritocracy framing.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
